english: representations of lotos, sun and moon. — pad-ma-pa-ni (pad ma pa ni) lotos-bearer, name of Awalokiteswara, Köpp. II, 23. — Pad-ma-byuṅ gnas (pad ma byung gnas), Sskr. P. Sambhava, also: U-rgyan-pád-ma (u rgyan pad ma), one of the most famous divines and holy magicians, in the 8th century, from Urgyén (u rgyan) (Ssk. Udayana) i.e. Kabul, who acc. to his own declaration (v. the fantastic legend concerning him, entitled: pad-ma taṅ-yig (pad ma tang yig)) was greater than Buddha himself, v. Köpp. II, 68. — pad-ma-ra-ga (pad ma ra ga) Ssk. ruby. — pád-rtsa (pad rtsa) a medicinal herb Wdṅ (= pe-tsé? (pe tse?)).

Ssk., Pandit, Indian scholar or linguist; pan-čén (pan chen) great Pandit; pan-čén rin-po-čé (pan chen rin po che), bog-do (bog do) (Mongolian) rin-čén (rin chen), title of the second Buddhist pope, residing at Tashilunpo, Köpp. II, 121. — pan-ža (pan zha) Pandit-cap.
(also pan-pún? (pan pun?)) not considered perfect in dignity, as for instance the Lamas in Lh., that are married; yet cf. ban-bón (ban bon).

I. form, mould, blugs-par (blugs par) casting-mould; rdéu-par (rde'u par) bullet-mould; blúgs-par (blugs par), as well as šiṅ-par (shing par), printing form, a stereotype plate cut in wood; par rkó-ba (par rko ba) to cut types; rgyáb-pa (rgyab pa), par-du ꜝdébs-pa (par du 'debs pa), to print, to stamp; par (-yig) ꜝbrí-ba (par yig 'bri ba) to write the exemplar or manuscript for printing. — pár-rko-pa (par rko pa), pár-rko-mkan (par rko mkhan), cutter of types. — pár-kaṅ (par khang) printing-office. — par-rgyáb (par rgyab) print, *par-rgyáb tsógs-so* (par rgyab tshogs so) W. like a print or impression. — par-snág (par snag) printing-ink. — pár-pa (par pa) printer Cs. — pár-dpon (par dpon) fore-man of a printing-office. — pár-ma (par ma) a printed work, book; 'di pár-ma yaṅ yod ('di par ma yang yod) this is also to be had printed. — par-ɣyóg (par g.yog) a printer's man, assistant. — par-šóg (par shog) printing-paper. — par-ɣži (par gzhi) = par (par). — II. v. pár-ma (par ma). — III. termin. of pa (pa), also sign of the adverb; combined with verbs, it represents the supine, or adverbial sentences, commencing with whilst, so that; mi byéd-par (mi byed par) without doing.
Lex., a hairy carpet Sch.
n. of an officinal plant Med.
Lexx., Sch. = pa-tra (pa tra).
W. a kind of cotton cloth.
Hind. scales of a balance Sik.

1. the instr. of pa (pa); combined with verbs, it signifies by, in consequence of, because; also as, since, when. — 2. = las (las), as sign of the comparative; after vowels, however and the final consonants d, r, l, bas (bas) stands in its place; rtá-bas kyi čuṅ-ba yin (rta bas khyi chung ba yin) the dog is smaller than the horse; kyód-pas (khyod pas), stág-pas (stag pas), rtá-pa-bas (rta pa bas), sṅár-bas (sngar bas), or sṅá-ma-bas (snga ma bas) ce (che), bigger than you, than a tiger, than a rider, than formerly; it rarely stands for the partitive: bu lṅa-brgyá-bas ɣčig (bu lnga brgya bas gcig), or for las (las) with the signification: except, Mil.
num. fig.: 43.
(Turk. پچاق) large butcher's knife.
1. Schr., Sch. fife, flute. — 2. W. nipple, teat; *pi-pi nud táṅ-če* (pi pi nud tang ce) to suckle. — 3. icicle W.
Ssk. पिप्पली. Piper longum, a spice, similar to black pepper, yet more oblong.
v. pi-ši (pi shi).
skin, or leather bag for water etc. Lh.
and ma-tsí (ma tsi), interjections of anger, Foucaux Gyatch. 284, transl. 292.

or pi-báṅ (pi bang), Zam. = वीणा, guitar, also da-nyín-pi-waṅ (da nyin pi wang) C., pi-waṅ ról-mo (pi wang rol mo) Glr. = kó-poṅ (ko pong) W.; pi-waṅ rgyud gsum (pi wang rgyud gsum) a three-stringed guitar Stg.; rgyud-máṅ (rgyud mang) a guitar with many strings Cs.; sgróg-pa (sgrog pa) to play (the guitar); pi-waṅ-mkan (pi wang mkhan), or pi-waṅ-pa (pi wang pa) a player on the guitar.
(perh. from the Persian) cat, W.; pi-pó (pi po) male cat, pi-mó (pi mo) female cat.
v. pús-mo (pus mo).
brush, pencil; byúg-pir (byug pir) large brush, for house-painting; bčád-pir (bcad pir) small
